lessons.title: What is your name?
lessons.topic: Identity and the sounds of F and G

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet a new friend?
questions.hint: It's the first thing you say to someone.

questions.question_text: What sound does the word 'Frog' start with?
What do you say at the end of a fun conversation?

steps.title: Funny Goat
steps.text: Hello! My name is Anna. I am a girl. This is my goat. He is a funny goat. I have a fish too.

steps.explanation: Use 'This is my' to show what you have.

steps.audio_text: Hi! I'm Anna. This is my goat. She has a funny name.

steps.prompt: Say: My name is [Your Name] and I have a goat.
steps.expected_text: my name is [your name] and i have a goat

questions.question_text: My name is ___. (Grammar question)
Which word has a beginning sound like 'Fish'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: A goat is a type of animal.
What animal does Tom have?

lessons.title: He and She
lessons.topic: Gender pronouns and the sound of H

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What is the girl holding in her hand?
questions.hint: It floats in the air when filled with gas.

questions.question_text: Who is wearing a yellow shirt?
What is the first thing the girl says to the boy?

steps.title: The Park
steps.text: My name is Tom. He is Jack. He is my friend. She is Lily. She has a red hat. This is my favorite park.

steps.explanation: Use 'He' for boys and 'She' for girls.

steps.audio_text: Hi, this is Tom. He is wearing a big blue hat. Tom smiles because he sees his best friend, Lucy.

questions.question: Who is Tom happy to see?

questions.question_text: What should the boy wear on his head? (Vocabulary question)
He ___ happy. (Grammar question)
Who is the girl with the red dress? (Reading comprehension)
True or False: 'She' can be used for a boy.
Which sound does 'H' make at the start of 'hat'?

lessons.title: Meet My Friend
lessons.topic: Introduction of others and sounds of I and J

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What is the first letter you hear in the word 'igloo'?
Which letter sounds like the start of 'jolly'?
What letter begins the word 'insect'?
questions.hint: It starts with the same letter as 'igloo'.

steps.title: Leo and the Jet
steps.text: This is my friend. His name is Leo. He is happy in an igloo! Look at the jet! It flies fast.

steps.explanation: Use 'He is my' to introduce a boy.

steps.audio_text: Hello! I'm Isla, and this is my igloo. My friend Jack is excited to visit.

questions.question_text: This is my ___. (Use a lesson vocabulary word)
Which word starts with 'I'? (Vocabulary question)
What does Jack have? (Reading comprehension)
True or False: This is my igloo.
Complete the sentence: 'This is my ___ friend Jack.'

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What furry animal says 'woof' and starts with 'D'?
questions.hint: It loves to fetch and play with a ball.

questions.question_text: Which fruit is small, red, and starts with 'A'?
questions.hint: It can be red or green and keeps the doctor away.

questions.question_text: What is white, fluffy, and falls from the sky in winter?
questions.hint: You can make a snowman with it.

steps.title: My Name is Ben
steps.text: Hello! My name is Ben. I am a boy. This is Lily. She is my friend. We see a frog and a cat.

questions.question: What do Ben and Lily see?

steps.audio_text: Hello! I am Alex. My friend, Lucy, loves drawing and playing with her dog in the garden.

questions.question: What does Lucy love to do?
Where does Lucy play with her dog?

steps.prompt: Say: My name is Emma and this is my friend Jack.
steps.expected_text: my name is emma and this is my friend jack

questions.question_text: I ___ Ella. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'pencil'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'Goodbye' is a greeting.
He ___ a student. (Grammar question)
What is the name of Mia's friend? (Reading comprehension question)
